fully_connected_layer: A neural network layer where every neuron receives input from all neurons in the previous layer.
relu: An activation function (Rectified Linear Unit) that outputs zero for negative values and keeps positive values unchanged.
activation_function: A mathematical function applied to a neuron's output to introduce non-linear behavior, allowing the network to model complex relationships.
softmax: An activation function used in the output layer of a classification model that converts raw scores into probabilities for each class.
classification: A type of machine learning task where a model assigns an input to one of several predefined categories.
cross_entropy: A loss function used in classification that measures how different the predicted probability distribution is from the correct labels.
gradient_descent: An optimization algorithm that adjusts model parameters step by step to reduce prediction error.
backpropagation: An algorithm that computes how much each weight in the network contributed to the error by propagating the error backward through the layers.
weights: Adjustable numerical parameters in a neural network that determine how strongly signals between neurons influence each other.
MNIST: A dataset of 70,000 handwritten digit images (0-9). Each image is 28x28 grayscale and widely used for training and testing simple image classifiers.
Fashion_MNIST: A MNIST-format dataset of clothing images such as shirts, shoes, and bags. It keeps the same 28x28 grayscale format but is slightly harder to classify.
EMNIST: An extended version of MNIST that includes handwritten letters as well as digits. Some variants contain up to 62 character classes.
KMNIST: A MNIST-style dataset of classical Japanese cursive characters. It keeps the 28x28 grayscale format but is more visually complex.
